# Helmlab

> A data-driven analytical color space for UI design systems.

Helmlab is a family of two purpose-built color spaces: MetricSpace for perceptual distance measurement, and GenSpace for gradient/palette generation. It is open-source (MIT), available as both a Python (pip) and JavaScript/TypeScript (npm) package.

- Website: https://helmlab.space
- GitHub: https://github.com/Grkmyldz148/helmlab
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/helmlab
- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/helmlab/
- Paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.23010
- Documentation: https://helmlab.space/docs.html
- Interactive Demo: https://helmlab.space/demo.html
- Design Tools: https://helmlab.space/tools.html
- Author: Gorkem Yildiz

## What It Does

Helmlab provides:
- Accurate perceptual color difference measurement (STRESS 23.30 on COMBVD, 20% better than CIEDE2000)
- Perfectly uniform gradient generation (CV ~ 0% via CIEDE2000 arc-length reparameterization)
- WCAG contrast ratio checking and enforcement
- Gamut mapping (sRGB, Display P3)
- Palette generation (Tailwind-style semantic scales, lightness ramps, hue rings)
- Token export (CSS oklch(), Android XML, iOS Swift, Tailwind, JSON)

## Architecture

### MetricSpace (72 parameters)
Optimized for perceptual distance. 13-stage enriched pipeline:
XYZ -> M1 -> power -> M2 -> hue correction -> H-K -> L scaling -> chroma scaling -> neutral correction -> rotation -> Lab

Best use: deltaE(), color difference measurement, accessibility checking, color harmony validation.

### GenSpace (18 parameters)
Optimized for generation. v14 CMA-ES optimized, wins 28/43 benchmarks vs OKLab (6/43). Minimal linear-algebra pipeline:
XYZ -> M1 -> cube root -> M2 -> neutral correction -> Lab
Plus CIEDE2000 arc-length reparameterization for gradient().

Best use: gradient(), palette(), semanticScale(), any color generation task.

## Installation

Python:
```
pip install helmlab
```

JavaScript/TypeScript:
```
npm install helmlab
```

## Quick Start (JavaScript)

```js
import { Helmlab } from 'helmlab';
const hl = new Helmlab();

hl.fromHex('#3B82F6');                          // Hex -> MetricSpace Lab
hl.toHex([0.5, -0.1, 0.2]);                    // Lab -> hex (gamut mapped)
hl.deltaE('#ff0000', '#00ff00');                // Perceptual distance
hl.gradient('#ff0000', '#0000ff', 8);           // Perfectly uniform gradient
hl.contrastRatio('#ffffff', '#3B82F6');          // WCAG contrast ratio
hl.ensureContrast('#3B82F6', '#ffffff', 4.5);   // Adjust to meet 4.5:1
hl.semanticScale('#3B82F6');                     // Tailwind-style 50-950 scale
hl.palette('#3B82F6', 5);                       // Lightness ramp
```

## Quick Start (Python)

```python
from helmlab import Helmlab
hl = Helmlab()

hl.from_srgb([0.2, 0.5, 0.8])                  # sRGB -> MetricSpace Lab
hl.delta_e('#ff0000', '#00ff00')                # Perceptual distance
hl.gradient('#ff0000', '#0000ff', 8)            # Perfectly uniform gradient
hl.ensure_contrast('#ffffff', '#3B82F6', 4.5)   # WCAG contrast enforcement
hl.semantic_scale('#3B82F6')                    # Tailwind-style 50-950 scale
```

## Benchmarks

### Perceptual Distance (MetricSpace)
STRESS on COMBVD (3,813 color pairs), lower is better:
- Helmlab v20b: 23.30 (best)
- CIEDE2000: 29.18
- CIE94: 33.59
- CAM16-UCS: 33.90
- CIE Lab dE76: 42.80
- Oklab: 47.46

### Gradient Uniformity
CV of CIEDE2000 step sizes, lower is better:
- Helmlab gradient(): ~0% on any color pair (arc-length reparameterization)
- Helmlab GenSpace linear: 3.1% / 33.2% / 41.0% (Red-Blue / Orange-Cyan / Black-White)
- Oklab linear: 31.5% / 41.4% / 41.2%
- CIE Lab linear: 44.8% / 52.3% / 61.5%
Note: gradient()'s ~0% comes from CIEDE2000 arc-length reparameterization, not the space itself. The same technique could be applied to any space; Helmlab ships it built-in.

## Package Details
- JavaScript: ~12KB gzipped, zero dependencies, ESM + CJS, full TypeScript types
- Python: requires numpy, scipy
- License: MIT
